reviews.text: Would not recommend treatment here. They tell you whatever you want to hear to get you signed up. They also told me that along with the test and trying a new product that I didn’t know if I’d do well with, I could cancel anytime free of charge they just contact the financial company and close the account. It turns out the this was not true. And I’m stuck with a $3,900 balance. Modern day con-artists playing with your health and wallet. reviews.text: I had a great experience at Tri-State Men's Health. The staff took the time to thoroughly explain my lab results, treatment options, and what to expect with testosterone therapy. I never felt rushed, and they made sure all of my questions were answered. I feel confident and well-supported starting this new phase of my health journey.

reviews.name: places/ChIJ430BZLebQIgRIQVGYFAimy0/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xkcmFUVm1YM2RtTmxsMVVIQk1XbGxwTmt0ZlMwRRAB
reviews.text:
We were recommended by a friend who is a patient.

My husband became a patient about a week ago. From the Scheduling process to the medical examination/lab work/recommendations even to financing it was one of the best experiences I’ve ever had in a medical office. The gentleman up front Marc made things extremely smooth was very warm, and made us feel comfortable ( I mean let’s face it if you’re looking into a facility that deals with men’s health it’s not always the most comfortable feeling internally and nerves were a little tense driving over to the apt. ) Marc offered a smile and a coffee and got us seen so quickly I was still putting creamer in my coffee as we were heading back to be examined. All of the nursing staff had exceptional bed side manner and were super professional and educational when going over results, and recommendations/ treatment plan they made everything super understandable. The gentleman in financing was also exceptional. He broke everything down for us and explained every step of the billing and what is covered/included was also very funny and knowledgeable about everything. He also made sure the financing option that we chose was the right fit for us.

Since the visit my husband has had MAJOR improvements and changes within just a week! From his lifts in the gym, and recovering from them to just overall motivation, and happiness throughout the day. We are so grateful for this establishment, and what they provide to the community. We have also had an open line of communication with the office for any questions and follow up. They’ve checked in with us. Marc has reached out to see how things were going and to also update us on all the tracking information for medications which were delivered timely,(one of them came the next morning the 2nd came the day after that ) both packaged very nicely with proper information and prescription info included.

The office was also in a very nice building and was clean organized and very aesthetically pleasing.

10/10 would recommend to anyone Experiencing situations that this health facility provides care for.

Can’t say enough great things about this place.

reviews.name: places/ChIJ430BZLebQIgRIQVGYFAimy0/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT210d05YSkhTRWhFTUZKNVFrWjFUakpuTnpRdFJWRRAB
reviews.text: Thank you Tri-State! You’ve been the biggest blessing. My husband  started going there about a year ago and I have noticed a huge positive change for him. The staff is so warm and welcoming. Traci was so informative and helped my husband to understand his options. They took some blood and helped cater his treatment plan to what exactly was needed. We sat down with the treatment coordinator, Evan, and he explained everything so thoroughly. We didn’t feel pressured or like we were being pushed treatment. He told us what to expect, that it takes time to get things exactly right, the cost and even cancellation policy. It took a little time to get everything just right. I really couldn’t be happier that we did this. I got my husband back! He has energy and confidence again! Like he was when we first met 15 years ago and I love it! Ladies, it’s the gift that keeps on giving!

reviews.text: What an inviting business! They greet you with a smile and make the conversation very easy. I was recommended to Optimize-U after learning that my previous provider could no longer subscribe TRT out of state. The ladies at Optimize you have been great to work with! They are able to do lab draws in house, gave me a detailed status of hormone, cholesterol, kidney, and liver function etc. With my membership we were able to address where my body is at, and set a road map for where I want to be.  After my bloodwork and review they introduced me to the recovery clinic, which hosts various wellness bays. They have red light therapy, infared sauna, a cold plunge, and my new favorite thing, a cryochamber! Talk about feeling alive! Cryo feels incredible! Im happy to have a provider close to home for once, and to have so many perks that I can use weekly! Oh, and they offered rates for military, which is nice since the military refuses to recognize the benefits of hormone replacement. Anyway, 10 out of 10 must recommend! I've already sent a group chat to my golf buddies because I know they could use professional help! Thanks Optimize-U, I look forward to our continued work together!

reviews.text:
I cannot recommend Nurse Practitioner Vanessa Ramirez at The Happy Hormone Cottage enough!
After a clinical PCOS diagnosis this year and a lifetime of excruciating periods (since 5th grade), I was facing exploratory surgery for suspected endometriosis. I never felt peace about it, so I canceled. My GYN only offered surgery, birth control, gabapentin, or metformin. So instead, I did a DUTCH test for a bigger picture, found Vanessa, and got an Oura ring through my HSA to better track cycles!
With her compassionate, expert care, I started progesterone therapy and targeted PCOS supplements. My painful periods have dramatically improved, and after years of infertility, tears, and prayers, my husband and I are overjoyed to be expecting our miracle baby!  “For this child I prayed; and the LORD has granted me my petition which I made to him.” (1 Samuel 1:27). God is so good!
Vanessa listens, truly cares, and treated me like a friend, not just a chart. If you’re struggling with PCOS, hormone issues, painful periods, or infertility, run --don't walk-- to see Vanessa! You’ll be so glad you did. Thank you from the bottom of our hearts, HHC & VR! 💕

reviews.name: places/ChIJMQ_9OcsDMYgRc0witYXDUE4/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNYNWZlZVdBEAE
reviews.text: I have been a patient of Dr. Patel since 2018 when I very suddenly came down with a mysterious illness and strange symptoms.  I felt terrible and didn't know where to turn for help after seeing multiple doctors who did nothing for me.  When my family physician told me "it was all in my head", I finally ended up at Dr. Patel's office thanks to a friend suggesting I try functional medicine.  She listened to me and asked so many questions.  I finally felt respected and cared for.  She took the time to really get to know me and delved into finding the root cause of all my symptoms.  It took some time, effort and patience but I got so much better and I will be forever grateful to her.  I moved to Colorado a few years ago and just reconnected with her in Ohio on my last visit.  Dr. Patel has changed my life for the better and she has my utmost respect.  I recommend her to anyone who is looking to improve their health and well being so they can live life to its fullest.

reviews.name: places/ChIJMQ_9OcsDMYgRc0witYXDUE4/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnTUNRbUpyMVpBEAE
reviews.text:
Unfortunately, despite many positive recommendations, I had a very bad experience with Dr. Patel. In our initial appointment, she told me that “fear and anxiety are large parts of Long Covid.” This unfortunately mimics psychologizing and misogynistic language about Long Covid that prevented me from getting proper care as a former college athlete. She also repeatedly recommended brain retraining, telling me that my “mast cells are fearful” when I mentioned that large blood draws had caused me crashes in the past. When I asked a question about why she recommended a particular treatment she said I was very sick, and “[my] body is losing.”

I also gave the practice feedback on improving the elimination diet resources (giving education on how to get nutritional needs properly met and maintaining a positive relationship with food during an elimination diet) as well as improving the almost unreadable patient notes. The front office promptly responded to this feedback.

Dr. Patel did do some helpful tests and had some ideas about addressing mold in our home and adding certain supplements that were useful; however, the harmful language she used, lack of appropriate guidance on the elimination diet, and lack of awareness of what it means to live with an energy-limiting illness meant that my symptoms worsened significantly as her patient. I hope that my feedback can be used to improve the quality of care given so that other patients do not have to go through the same thing.

I am very grateful to have Dr Patel as my doctor!

25Again has completely changed my life.  I'm a 54yo guy who works in banking (not a lot of exercise in that), and I had been feeling the slow drag and degrade of time on my body, mind and spirit.  Grind in, grind out.  As a former athlete, I know what fitness looks like, and the guy in the mirror staring back at me was NOT the depiction of fitness.  Mirror mirror on the wall, who's that stranger in the hall?

That all changed about 3mos after I started the program.  I've been on the program now for 6yrs (sorry it took so long to leave a review), and folks, let me tell you, THE PROGRAM WORKS!  Is this a miracle cure?  Almost!  But it takes dedication, time, discipline and commitment.   IT'S A PLAN!  And sticking TO the plan gave me the results I had hoped for.  Energy, libido, stamina, desire, strength, focus, clarity.  The program for me checked all the boxes I needed.  And my wife... oh my poor wife!  8yrs my junior and she can barely keep up, and she's on the program too!!!  I feel bad for our bed frame (and sofas, and stairwell, and back seat of car, and countertops, and dining room table).   Just kidding!!! (only kinda).

It goes without saying, a big thank you goes out to Erin & Kasey.  You ladies really made it happen!  I'm forever indebted.

~Mike

reviews.text:
So, where do I even start? Stay clear of this place is the first thing I will say.

My boyfriend went here for ED. Plan was to go to his urologist and get medication. He heard this place on the radio and was just amazed by their ad on the radio and the “free consult” also the “affordable treatment” so he went this direction. What a bad decision this was! His appointment was at 2:30 he didn’t get home till after 4;00 okay they did do a lot and also gave him a treatment. So long appointment I guess makes sense. However when he was at his appointment they discussed everything with him and gave him prices for the treatment and so forth my boyfriend mistakenly heard the guy say the price was $82 the guy took his card to run it so start this “affordable” treatment. When the guy came back and said it didn’t go through and my boyfriend asked wait how much did you try to take the guy then says $8,200!!!! This is affordable treatment for who? With that price certainly not affordable for us so he told them he wouldn’t be taking the treatment and left. About 5:30 he realizes they kept his debit card! So he calls the place and their ever so pleasant receptionist tell him the guy that would have it isn’t available now he would call him back later no we need the card so my boyfriend tells them well I’ll come get it tomorrow she informs him they close at 6 for the weekend. He says then I’m heading there I can’t go all weekend without my card this woman tells him he better hurry they close and lock the doors at 6pm. Okay he failed to return his card so be a little more friendly and understanding and offer to stay open till we get there? Nope she was rude! We luckly got there with 5 mins to spare. So here it is 9:00pm and this treatment they did has not warn off yet so he’ does the steps the paper tells him to do incase of emergency and it not going down. We are over 6 hours of him being up. So he calls the emergency line and this lady that answers probably the receptionist seems like she’s on another phone and is being interrupted by the “emergency phone call” she had to take. She tells him to do what the paper says mind you he has already taken the steps so he goes along with. And of course it didn’t work so he calls them back and gets someone else. They tell him to head to the office and someone would meet him there so here we are again for the THIRD time now driving to the office 25 mins away. So by this time it’s 9:30pm gets there and can’t even open the door!!! So he gets a call and my b/f tells him at this point it’s been over 6 hours they tell him head to the ER it will take an hour before someone can get out there go unlock the door! And he didn’t want him to wait much longer let because apparently at 3hrs it’s a problem. Someone was supposed to call him after a few hours and check up on him never received a phone call from anyone This has got to be the most unprofessional place I have ever dealt with! So here it is 11:30pm and still at the ER doctor already taken out blood twice and given 2 doses of medicine! 12:30am finally released with him having to see urologist in 3 days.  They should be paying gas for the back and forth so many times, the hospital and urology bill since he now has to go there to see if there is more damage.  There are many other legit places out there do your research.

reviews.text: I am writing this review for dad. He got a stem cell injection last year.  He has been an avid golfer all his life (he's in his 70's).  His back pain had gotten to the point that he was contemplating cancelling a golf trip to Arizona and possibly retiring his golf clubs!  He had the injection about a month before his trip. reviews.text: Sad to say, stem cell injections don't work for everybody. My ten thousand dollar mistake. reviews.name: places/ChIJMYrZuh2POIgRtTqQ0YRUCrM/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNEd3VUOFl3EAE
reviews.text: I found Cenegenics by searching for a local professional who could help me figure out different health issues I have been experiencing. The process started off with a phone call and on that phone call the woman explained that I was younger than clients they normally worked with but she definitely thought I was a candidate and someone who could benefit from their program. I am 33 and have been experiencing weight gain despite exercise and diet. Along with other hormonal imbalances. I didn't want to find another doctor who would run tests, find that I was within normal ranges but not help me figure out why I wasn't feeling like myself and rapidly gaining weight. I moved forward with the $1,500 comprehensive blood draw because I felt like it was going to give us a good look at why I was feeling the way that I was and I was finally going to receive guidance and a path towards feeling well again. The blood work came back and I was at the bottom of normal range and low in some other hormones. When I had a video call with the doctor, which was not from Columbus, he said my Vitamin D levels were very low and thought it would be best for me to take a supplement. Despite everything I told him I was going through and my results, his only advice was to work harder. Now I am the type of person when told to work harder I will. Thankfully I love nutrition and learning new things. I dove deep into my results and have been taking supplements, different herbal medicines to support my hormone health and exercising differently since those results were posted in mid January. I'm starting to feel better and I'm not gaining and have actually lost a few pounds plus feel my body composition beginning to change. I say this to tell you that Cenegenics does not work with everyone and if I would have known what their approach to my results was going to be, I never would have begun testing with them. I would have went with someone else. Although I'm extremely grateful that I did the blood work and could work from those results on my own. Just thought this could be helpful for a young woman in my situation who is seeking answers. I would not recommend this program.

reviews.text: I have been a patient of Dr. Christofides for five years and I tell her at the end of every appointment how thankful I am for her expert care. At my first appointment in 2020 she told me that in one year I would be “boring,” which for her meant stable. I recommend Dr. Christofides to everyone I meet who needs good endocrine support.

NOW YOU KNOW THE WHO.....figure out the how and when!!

reviews.text:
Everyone could benefit by going to Dr. Elana Christofides!! She is that good. I often pass her info along to friends, family, students and clients (I facilitate ageless health and wellness education). Originally, I was seeking the option of bioidentical hormones. She listened. reviews.text:
For the past five years I struggled with chronic fatigue and nausea so severe that at times I couldn’t even leave my home. Because I was in my early 20s, most doctors brushed it off and never took a deeper look.

I started my journey with Metabolab just a month ago, and I’ve already experienced incredible improvements. I haven’t had this much energy since I was a teenager, and I’ve even started gaining muscle. For the first time, I feel like I’m being truly heard.

Dr. Bartlett takes the time to dive into every detail, and Metabolab offers access to testing I didn’t even know existed. Both Dr. Bartlett and Nick are so kind, patient, and thorough — they explain everything clearly and are always available to answer my questions.

What I love most is that they don’t just treat symptoms — they work to uncover the root cause. At 25, I wasn’t sure if this would be the right fit for me, but it’s honestly the best decision I’ve made for my health. If you want to improve your quality of life, Metabolab is the place to go. I couldn’t recommend them enough.

reviews.text:
This review reflects my personal experience during an appointment with Dr. Alexander-Martin.

This was one of the most disturbing medical encounters I have ever had. From the outset, any attempt to ask questions or participate in my own care was shut down. When I raised concerns about medications being prescribed and referenced the actual FDA labeling, the response was not clarification or discussion, but outright dismissal. There was no willingness to engage with evidence or explain clinical reasoning. Input from the patient was clearly unwelcome and seemed like she took any input personally.

The interaction was tense and adversarial. The tone was defensive and combative, and I felt spoken to in a way that was dismissive and degrading rather than professional. Asking reasonable, informed questions was treated as a problem instead of a normal and appropriate part of medical care.

What was most alarming was the complete lack of openness to being questioned or corrected, even when objective information was presented. That alone made me deeply uncomfortable trusting any aspect of the care being provided.

To compound the experience, the billing felt excessive and disproportionate to the quality of care and communication I received, leaving me with serious concerns about priorities at this practice.

I left feeling stressed, unheard, and shaken by how poorly I was treated for simply advocating for myself. Patients who value evidence-based medicine, respectful communication, and being treated with basic dignity should think very carefully before scheduling here.

reviews.text: I have been going here for years and years. I have had a membership for years and years. I have always loved it there. Rebecca is amazing and I love her! Recently though, within this past year things have gone really downhill. First they moved their office into the gym… which is not ideal. It smells horrible and it feels really unprofessional for a doctors office. Also getting in for an appointment is insane. For years I paid for the membership because if I was sick I could usually get in the same day. If I had something else going on I could get in that week or the next. Now if I’m sick I can’t get in for 2 weeks, and anything else I have to wait over a month. I used to be able to just call in same day to come in and get some IV fluids if I wanted. Just a nurse appointment, quick and easy. Now I can never get in. The membership prices go up and the care is still great if you can get it. I’ve had to go to the minute clinic twice because I couldn’t get in for weeks when I was sick. Why pay a membership price for a concierge doctors office if it’s not concierge service anymore? It’s really disappointing.

reviews.name: places/ChIJPZ_Cpa2POIgRs8H8B20Bev4/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnTUNneTZLRHhnRRAB
reviews.text:
I strongly advise against visiting Dr. Bailey and his team. They provide little to no time for patients, offer minimal assistance with issues, and seem more focused on internal procedures than actual patient care. Dr. Bailey frequently asked me what the next step should be instead of offering his own medical expertise, which was both frustrating and unprofessional.

This office also refuses to provide direct referrals, forcing you to schedule unnecessary appointments and spend hundreds of dollars just to have a brief conversation with Dr. Bailey. Additionally, their administrative incompetence caused serious delays in my care. Last year, they failed to properly send documentation to another office, turning what should have been a two-week process into a six-month ordeal.

The staff is poorly trained, difficult to reach, and experiences constant turnover, making consistency in care nearly impossible. They appear more concerned with maintaining their image than actually helping their patients. Frankly, I'm shocked this office is still operating given how inefficient and unhelpful they’ve been. Red flags everywhere—avoid at all costs.

reviews.text: Just read a review where a woman had voice changes, I had the same.   Dr Glasser speeds thru so fast and does’t listen to my concerns. She had my Testosterone over 1000.  To a point my hair was falling out.  Told her the issues after the first raised dose cycle. She had raised my dose with zero medical testing or reason  to show I needed it.  She then again gave me the same dose the next visit after my concerns  that lasted another 3-4 months to extreme Hair loss. She claims it does not cause hair loss but oddly there is literature all around her office about combatting hair loss.  I feel she doesn’t test blood often enough and we are run thru  like an assembly line all day, no insurance taken just $$$.

reviews.name: places/ChIJ0UPjdu6OQIgR9ioiQuKHHas/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNCM3FDRW1RRRAB
reviews.text: It took my  a year to write this and I feel some guilt in doing so, but people need to know. I received hormone pellets for over a year. I had labs drawn initially but then no follow up labs were ordered or performed for a year. So I go in and tell her I think I need to back down the dose. I explained that I was having some negative side effects including voice changes. She was applauded that I suggested the hormones could change my voice. She told me I would more likely have throat cancer than voice changes related to testosterone pellets. I scheduled a throat ultrasound and everything. I moved and had to find a new doctor. Before he would administer pellets he said he needed to do labs. He was also very surprised by the large amount of testosterone I was receiving. He called the next day with my lab results. My testosterone levels were insanely high and my estrogen was zero. He automatically said you have to be having voice changes and other negative side effects!! Dr. Glaser made feel ridiculous for questioning the effects of testosterone. My voice is much better after being off the testosterone for months but sometimes the effects are permanent. Listen to your body. Ask the questions and if you are made to feel stupid, talk to someone else.

reviews.text: Dr Glasser decided to up my  testosterone when I had zero problem on my current dose. I told her at my next visit my hair was falling out.  She gave me the huge dose again and it got worse.  These are 3 month pellets. .  When it wore off the hair loss stopped. Had no medical documentation  to up the dose. There is a reason why she changed her field of practice? I’d  Beware!!!!! We all make mistakes but it’s how you rectify it. She zooms too fast in and out of those appointments not hearing or not caring maybe? Shame on her. She only had to say , I am sorry. I made a mistake instead of denying it.  It was her not taking the dose back down after my first issue with it , then after the second time denying it

reviews.name: places/ChIJL3t0W9aIQIgREVcKhGWOgak/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNKeU5tWEVREAE
reviews.text:
Dr. Glaser gave me my first pellets 2009!  Many DO NOT know this, she did the research, wrote the white paper and created the scoreable in-basket test to determine a patient's symptoms.  From this survey, she determines what you need just as she did for me!  I originally sought pellet therapy to manage my RA pain.   The real game changer unexpectedly was no more hot flashes, and ok... intercourse was great again.   Testosterone therapy is so important for women for so many reasons!  It's also about cardiovascular and brain health.  Do your own research but Dr. Glaser is a pioneer!

The reception staff has always been kind and courteous and April, her NP, is very knowledgeable and capable and has administered my pellets on several occasions.   I moved to Pittsburgh and would drive home to Dayton until 2018 to get my my pellets.  I've moved to Florida now and found someone who can provide my therapy but no one will be Dr. Glaser.   Much adoration...

I had to pull my car over because I was so dissapointed about not getting any help with my post menopause.

reviews.text:
I don’t typically write reviews, but when I see one that doesn’t match my experience, I have to speak up. Gameday Men's Health truly has it together.

From the start, their communication and organization stood out. I get appointment confirmations by text and email before I even leave the office. The Maineville team is professional, warm, and genuinely cares about their patients.

Let’s be honest, guys, if you’re looking into this, it’s because you’ve been feeling tired, foggy, weak, or just not yourself. That was me. I did my initial consult, saw my testosterone levels were extremely low, and worked with Madison on a treatment plan that felt right for me.

Within a few weeks, the all day dragging fatigue no matter how much caffeine I drank was gone. I feel sharper, stronger, and more like myself again.

I’ve seen some people complain about insurance, but honestly, Gameday makes the process simple and stress free. They’re upfront about pricing, offer a free consultation, and never pressure you. For me, it’s been a hassle free path to getting my energy and health back on track.

reviews.text: Since becoming a patient at The Happy Hormone Cottage, my health has been much improved. I was referred by a neighbor who had success here and she was right to recommend them. I recall an initial informational zoom meeting with was a great way to go over some of their services. I decided to move forward first with a thyroid evaluation. Cherie Gee was great. You won't get that kind of one on one time at a regular doctors office. She listened to my symptoms thoroughly and explained her recommendations. The thyroid panel she ordered was more extensive than what my previous provider had ordered over the YEARS and it was discovered that I have Hashimoto's Thyroiditis. She switched me from Synthroid to a compound T3,T4 and it made a world of difference. We've also implemented several supplements as well. I decided later to pursue the Dutch Test hormone panel and have since addressed deficiencies I had as well. Overall I feel physically much better. Just as important though, is the need for a patient to feel seen and heard. Because of the level of care I've received at The Happy Hormone Cottage, I feel like my health matters to them. reviews.name: places/ChIJJ-eqSQlXQIgRCaspsDrThVw/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xkVVJXcEdUMU5JWmkxSU4xUklValpqVFZneldHYxAB
reviews.text:
I must say that I am absolutely livid! Yesterday was my 6th visit and 6th shot with this Center. I will tell you that I've been very happy with the results and was really looking forward to getting past the sixth shot, getting my blood work up to date and moving forward. However, there is one nurse there that has been giving me my shots and doing my blood work for the most part probably 80% of the time. She is absolutely awful! Every time I've gotten injection from her it's hurt for the entire week at the injection point. The other shots I've gotten from two of the other nurses are literally no pain and no I'm going pain at all. Then, drawing blood for my test she dug up my arm so bad that I'm bruised once again big time. This happened the last time she took blood as well major bruising all in the middle of my arm cuz she digs around trying to find a vein. I have never had any issues giving blood before. And while you would expect a little bruise or something nothing like I experienced with her.

I voice my concerns yesterday when I went in. I ended up stopping the procedure because it was just too uncomfortable and I didn't want this woman touching me anymore. I'd pretty much resigned that I wasn't going to go back there anymore. But, with the success the treatments have given me I thought I would at least call them and try to work it out. I called and was reinsured by the doctor, Justin that this would not happen again and they would have sign a new nurse to me to take care of all those items. I was happy and satisfied with that and was ready to get right back in. My biggest concern is I never wanted to have that nurse anywhere near me again. So, I went in today to do the little blood work that I needed to have done and get my sixth injection. I checked in as normal I would have assumed that they would have put it on my file that I was not to see this lady anymore. Sure enough, and she walks again, the Butcher of Mason. It's like I hadn't said anything at all. I just got up and left.

Obviously they are not paying attention to their customers and patience needs and request, at all. They literally thumped their nose at mine. You would have thought that that would have been on my file. Super disappointed! Like unbelievably disappointed! Now I have to find another Center and start over.

When an absolute shame in what a utter disappointment. I called and spoke to Justin who was my doctor for this yesterday. He assured me that this wouldn't happen again and they would have another nurse take care of me. They didn't! Ignored me basically.

Super angry!!

Beware of the Butcher of Mason!

Follow up. To add insult to injury, the regional director for their centers reached out to me. Literally turned around on me....said it was "unfair" then I wrote a review after my experience and not giving them a chance to fix it. No apology. Nothing. Made it my fault. He said he wanted to make it right. They had a chance to make it right today. I was ready to let it go move forward and continue on with my treatment. They miss their opportunity. Horrifically!

I did give him a chance to fix it! I called yesterday. They said they would rectify the situation and it wouldn't happen again.  Needless to say, that didn't happen.

I have every right to be angry. Unfortunately, I really need this treatment. Now I'm going to have to try a new center. My insurance will no longer cover it because my levels are too high for the insurance companies now due to my treatments and I've already had. Basically this company is screwed me over and multiple way.

Unfortunately, I'm screwed.  These people suck!!

reviews.name: places/ChIJlV9pXmQdMYgRpJqIPCYyano/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT25GcFFWWlpSWFJMVFUxaE1IQmhWbGQ1YjNGNE9IYxAB
reviews.text:
My husband and I first saw Dr. Joseph in July 2025. Our initial appointment went very well — the consultation, prescriptions, and supplement recommendations helped us both feel great. We paid $400 each for that first visit, and were told this covered both the consultation and the prescriptions.

Unfortunately, everything after that point has been incredibly disappointing. Before our October follow-up appointments, I realized we never received our bloodwork referrals. When I called, the office insisted they had been sent and that I must have missed the email — even though I received every other message from UltaLabs. It was clear the referrals had simply never been done, and rather than owning the mistake, they blamed us. We had to delay our appointments while waiting for new labs, during which time my husband was without his medication for more than two weeks. Despite calls and emails alerting the Dr., the refill was never ordered.

At our rescheduled follow-ups on 10/21, we were charged $304 each. On 11/4, I contacted the office again because our prescriptions still hadn’t been received — turns out no one had entered the order. The next day, I was charged an additional $228 and $375, bringing the total for our October follow-ups to $1,211. When I reached out for clarification, the staff (specifically Sloan) repeatedly apologized and said we were “misinformed” about costs at our first visit. I eventually received refunds for the $228 and $375, but then was charged another $263 on 11/5 for medications from our July visit.

Through all of this, Dr. Joseph never once reached out to help resolve the situation or even acknowledge the ongoing confusion. The experience has left us feeling taken advantage of and deeply disappointed. What started as a promising experience turned into one of the most frustrating and disorganized medical experiences we’ve ever had.

I recently visited Gameday Men’s Health in Toledo, and the whole experience was outstanding from start to finish. The clinic is super clean—modern, well-organized, and feels professional yet welcoming right when you walk in. No cluttered waiting rooms or outdated vibes here.
The staff was incredibly friendly and polite. Everyone greeted me with a smile, took the time to explain things clearly without any rush or judgment, and made me feel comfortable discussing personal health concerns. Shoutout to the team—they really know how to put you at ease.
The process was impressively efficient. I got in and out quickly with same-day labs, a thorough consultation, and clear next steps. No long waits, no unnecessary red tape. They focus on men’s health needs (like TRT and wellness optimization) in a straightforward, results-oriented way.
If you’re in the Toledo area and considering men’s health services, Gameday is definitely worth checking out. Professional, discreet, and genuinely helpful. Feeling better already—thanks, team!

reviews.text: Visited Tri-State location in Westerville and had my T levels tested, Results came back low and started to discuss treatment options, Seemed appealing and what the NP explained they had great results and also discussed adding additional treatment. I started asking about cost or each treatment and was told its roughly  ... per month but someone will come in to discuss in detail. Then comes in the smiling closer...she described everything quickly and with the amazing results all of their patients have. I questioned things further and paying $2,999.00 for Testosterone injection once weekly and if I sign up for the 3 year plan it's only $5,999.00, plus the added NAD treatment for $2299.00 for 6 months. So 1 year would equate to roughly $5,300 ....and you sign a finance agreement that your bound to. Can't just cancel and walk away. Mind you that a physician can prescribe Testosterone injections for about $65 that lasts for 8-10 weeks. I left Tri-State without signing anything and went to my doctor next day and found out that there are better and way less expensive options. This is my personal experience so others may find this treatment options acceptable.
